steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: noun phrase (“a new country dialing code from the International Telegraph and Telephone Consultative Committee”).
After the blank: noun phrase (“incoming international telephone calls to reach the country”).

steps.title: Match the correct form
steps.content: A participle is used to modify a noun; a finite verb cannot (without a relative pronoun). Enabling is the participle form that correctly modifies the dialing code by describing its function. The other options are finite verbs (had enabled, would enable, enabled), which cannot function as noun modifiers in this position. That matches choice D — D is correct.
